seoTitle: How to Export Airtable as CSV Easily
metaDescription: Download any Airtable view as a CSV file by filtering the data first, then using the Download CSV option from the view menu.
intro: Exporting Airtable data as CSV is useful for backups or moving data into Excel or Google Sheets, and doing it from a specific filtered view means you only export what you actually need. This guide covers preparing your view and finding the Download CSV option.

steps.heading: Open your target table and view
steps.body: Navigate to the table and specific Grid View containing the data you want to export.

steps.heading: Apply filters and sorting
steps.body: Set view filters and sorting parameters so the export only includes the relevant row records.

steps.heading: Review and hide columns
steps.body: Hide any irrelevant columns before exporting to keep the resulting CSV clean.

steps.heading: Open the view configuration menu
steps.body: Click the view name dropdown arrow to access the core configuration tools.

steps.heading: Download the CSV
steps.body: Scroll down and select the 'Download CSV' action button to generate and save the file locally.

steps.heading: Verify and import elsewhere if needed
steps.body: Check your downloads folder for the file, then import it into Excel or Google Sheets for further analysis if needed.

faq.question: Does exporting a view as CSV include hidden fields?
faq.answer: No, hidden fields are typically excluded from the CSV export, which is why hiding irrelevant columns beforehand helps keep the file clean.

faq.question: Can I export just a filtered subset of my table?
faq.answer: Yes, applying filters and sorting to your view before exporting means only the filtered records are included in the CSV.

faq.question: Where does the exported CSV file go?
faq.answer: It downloads to your browser's default downloads folder like any other file download.

faq.question: Can I export linked record fields as CSV?
faq.answer: Linked record fields typically export as text representations of the linked records, though complex relational data may need Lookup fields for a cleaner export.
